prs.title: Fix: Back navigation freezing on /editor after adding tables (#687)
prs.body:
Fixes #687

**Summary:**
Fixes the issue where the browser’s Back button freezes or skips pages after adding a table in the /editor view.

**Problem:**
- Each autosave triggers `save()` in `Workspace.jsx`, which always calls `setSearchParams(searchParams);` even when no URL changes are needed.
- This pushes a new `/editor` entry to browser history on every save, causing the Back button to become unresponsive or require multiple presses.

**Solution:**
- Only call `setSearchParams()` when shareId exists in the URL.
- Use `{ replace: true }` to replace the current history entry instead of pushing new ones.

```
if (saveAsDiagram) {
  if (searchParams.has("shareId")) {
    searchParams.delete("shareId");
    setSearchParams(searchParams, { replace: true });
  }
}
```

**Result:**
- No duplicate /editor entries in browser history.
- Back navigation now works smoothly after adding tables.
- Autosave and URL updates remain fully functional.

**Verification:**
1. Navigate from / → /editor.
2. Add a new table (triggers autosave).
3. Press Back → returns immediately to /.
4. Repeat to confirm consistent behavior.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9727475e-c975-4290-a8c1-8cbedef07b0d


**Notes:**
This PR makes a minimal, targeted change to fix the root cause without affecting other functionality or state handling.

prs.title: Add TextInput layout issue example to RNTester (#54304)

labels.name: CLA Signed

labels.name: Stale
labels.description: There has been a lack of activity on this issue and it may be closed soon.

labels.name: Shared with Meta
labels.description: Applied via automation to indicate that an Issue or Pull Request has been shared with the team.

prs.body: ## Summary: This pull request adds a new example to **RNTester** named **`TextInputLayoutBugExample`**, which reproduces the layout issue in `TextInput` where multiline inputs with a fixed height and `textAlignVertical="top"` display text inconsistently on the first render. The example makes it easier for maintainers and contributors to visualize, debug, and verify fixes for the problem reported in #54304 ## Changelog: [INTERNAL] [ADDED] - Add TextInput layout issue example to RNTester (#54304) ## Test Plan: 1. Build and run RNTester on iOS. 2. Navigate to **Components → TextInput Layout Issue**. 3. Tap **“Long Note”** and observe that the text appears squashed near the top of the `TextInput`. 4. Tap **“Back”**, then open **“Short Note”**, and return to **“Long Note”** — the text now displays correctly, demonstrating the layout inconsistency. This confirms that the example reliably reproduces the issue described in (#54304) Attached below are two screenshots — one showing the bug (initial render) and another after navigation, where the text alignment corrects itself.

prs.title: Added Custom Scrollbar Styling to MemeCam Application
prs.body:
Summary:
This PR adds comprehensive scrollbar styling to enhance the MemeCam application's visual appeal and user experience. The implementation includes custom scrollbar themes that match the application's purple-pink gradient design.

Changes Made:
Custom Scrollbar Styling
WebKit Scrollbars: Custom styled scrollbars with purple-to-pink gradient theme
Firefox Support: Cross-browser compatibility with scrollbar-color and scrollbar-width
Interactive Effects: Hover animations with glow effects and smooth transitions
Theme Consistency: Scrollbars match the app's existing color scheme

Visual Enhancements:
Gradient Thumbs: Purple-to-pink gradient scrollbar thumbs (#8b5cf6 to #ec4899)
Hover Effects: Enhanced hover states with darker gradients and glow effects
Rounded Corners: Consistent border-radius for modern appearance
Smooth Transitions: 0.3s ease transitions for all scrollbar interactions

Responsive Design:
Mobile Optimization: Responsive scrollbar adjustments for smaller screens
Cross-Device Support: Consistent styling across desktop, tablet, and mobile
Viewport Awareness: Dynamic height calculations

Key Features:
Cross-Browser Compatibility: Works on Chrome, Safari, Firefox, and Edge
Accessibility: Maintains proper contrast and visibility
Performance: Lightweight implementation with minimal overhead
Consistency: Unified scrollbar appearance across all elements


Closes #5

Now both the Home Page and Footer components load by default.

issues.title: [BUG] Back navigation from /editor skips or freezes homepage
issues.body:
Describe the bug:
When navigating between the homepage (/) and the editor (/editor), the browser’s Back button behaves inconsistently.
Sometimes it:
1. Skips the homepage entirely and jumps back to the previous external page (like a Google search),
2. Does nothing and stays stuck on /editor, or
3. Requires multiple back presses to reach the homepage, and then forward navigation skips it again.

This makes navigation within the app unpredictable.

Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Search for “drawdb app” on Google.
2. Click the first link → opens the homepage (https://www.drawdb.app).
3. Click “Open Editor” to go to /editor.
4. Press the browser Back arrow (←).

Observe one of the following:

1. Homepage skipped → returns directly to Google.
2. Back button unresponsive.
3. Multiple presses required to reach homepage, and forward skips it again.

Expected behavior:
Pressing Back from /editor should always return to the homepage (/) before leaving the site.
Pressing Forward should go through the same in-app routes (homepage → editor) in order, without skipping.

Screenshot / Screen Recording
A short screen recording demonstrating all three navigation issues is attached below.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1d944a7a-8977-4b5b-b6a0-e7929f9a3c83

Desktop:
OS: macOS
Browser: Google Chrome (latest)
Reproducible: Yes (both normal and incognito mode)

}

issues.title: Create Complete Home Page for Campus Buddy
issues.body:
**Description:**
Design and implement the complete **Home Page** for the Campus Buddy website using **React** and **Tailwind CSS**. This page should use the already created reusable `Header` and `Footer` components for consistent layout.

---

### ✅ Tasks:

*  Import and use `Header` and `Footer` components
*  Create main content sections (e.g., Hero section, About Campus Buddy, Features, Testimonials, CTA, etc.)
*  Ensure responsiveness across devices
*  Follow the chosen color theme and UI design
*  Ensure all Tailwind classes follow best practices
*  Keep the layout clean and user-friendly
*  Add hover effects 
*  Use of libraries like reactbits, uiverse etc. is highly appreciated

---

### 📁 Folder Structure:

Place the home page file under:
`/src/pages/Home.jsx`

---

### 🔗 Dependencies:

* Must integrate smoothly with existing layout
* Should not break the navigation or overall design consistency

---

### 📸 Reference:

Use design references provided in the Whatsapp group or you can also take inspiration from designs on dribble

---

### 🧠 Notes:

* Focus on good spacing, padding, and visual hierarchy

---

issues.title: Design a Modern Logo for Campus Buddy
issues.body:
Create a clean, modern, text-based logo for the project “Campus Buddy”

🛠 Requirements:

Text should read: Campus Buddy
Keep it flat/minimal — no shadows or gradients
Create SVG or PNG version with transparent background
🎨 Design Guidelines:

Must look good on both light and dark backgrounds(can choose black/white)
Should be scalable (usable in navbar, favicon, hero section, etc.)
Keep it professional but friendly
📁 Output:

logo-light.svg / logo-dark.svg if versions differ
PNG version (for preview)

Toast Variants:

Login Successful-
type: success

Logged Out-

Event Created-

Event Deleted-

RSVP Failed / Limit Reached-

Profile Updated

issues.title: Create Footer Component with Dummy Content

type.name: Feature
type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality

issues.body:
> Build a reusable `Footer.jsx` component with dummy links and content using Tailwind CSS.

---

### 📌 **Tasks to be Done:**

*  Create a file `Footer.jsx` inside the `components/` folder
* Add 3–4 columns/sections with dummy headings like:

  * About
  * Quick Links
  * Socials
  * Contact
*  Add dummy links/text in each section (e.g., "Lorem ipsum", "#")
*  Use Tailwind to style the footer with:

  * Background color 
  * Text color
  * Padding and spacing
  * Grid or flex layout
*  Add a bottom line: `© 2025 Campus Buddy. All rights reserved.`
*  Make it responsive for mobile (stacked layout)

---

### 💡 Notes:

* No design is needed — focus on clean structure and responsiveness
* Can be updated later when Figma designs are ready
* Make it a reusable component that can be added to any page

---

issues.body: Add a pages folder inside src/ to organize each page/view like Home, About, Dashboard.

issues.title: Create components/ folder
issues.body: Add a components folder inside src/ to store all reusable UI components like buttons, cards, modals, etc.
